# bytes

```go
import "bytes"
```

Package bytes implements functions for the manipulation of byte slices. It is analogous to the facilities of the [strings](<https://pkg.go.dev/strings/>) package.

## Constants

<a name="MinRead"></a>MinRead is the minimum slice size passed to a [Buffer.Read](<#Buffer.Read>) call by [Buffer.ReadFrom](<#Buffer.ReadFrom>). As long as the [Buffer](<#Buffer>) has at least MinRead bytes beyond what is required to hold the contents of r, [Buffer.ReadFrom](<#Buffer.ReadFrom>) will not grow the underlying buffer.

```go
const MinRead = 512
```

## Variables

<a name="ErrTooLarge"></a>ErrTooLarge is passed to panic if memory cannot be allocated to store data in a buffer.

```go
var ErrTooLarge = errors.New("bytes.Buffer: too large")
```

<a name="Clone"></a>

## func [Clone](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1384>)

```go
func Clone(b []byte) []byte
```

Clone returns a copy of b\[:len\(b\)\]. The result may have additional unused capacity. Clone\(nil\) returns nil.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	b := []byte("abc")
	clone := bytes.Clone(b)
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", clone)
	clone[0] = 'd'
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", b)
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", clone)
}
```

#### Output

```go
abc
abc
dbc
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Compare"></a>

## func [Compare](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L28>)

```go
func Compare(a, b []byte) int
```

Compare returns an integer comparing two byte slices lexicographically. The result will be 0 if a == b, \-1 if a \< b, and \+1 if a \> b. A nil argument is equivalent to an empty slice.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
)

func main() {
	// Interpret Compare's result by comparing it to zero.
	var a, b []byte
	if bytes.Compare(a, b) < 0 {
		// a less b
	}
	if bytes.Compare(a, b) <= 0 {
		// a less or equal b
	}
	if bytes.Compare(a, b) > 0 {
		// a greater b
	}
	if bytes.Compare(a, b) >= 0 {
		// a greater or equal b
	}

	// Prefer Equal to Compare for equality comparisons.
	if bytes.Equal(a, b) {
		// a equal b
	}
	if !bytes.Equal(a, b) {
		// a not equal b
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Search)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"slices"
)

func main() {
	// Binary search to find a matching byte slice.
	var needle []byte
	var haystack [][]byte // Assume sorted
	_, found := slices.BinarySearchFunc(haystack, needle, bytes.Compare)
	if found {
		// Found it!
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Contains"></a>

## func [Contains](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L77>)

```go
func Contains(b, subslice []byte) bool
```

Contains reports whether subslice is within b.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.Contains([]byte("seafood"), []byte("foo")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.Contains([]byte("seafood"), []byte("bar")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.Contains([]byte("seafood"), []byte("")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.Contains([]byte(""), []byte("")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
true
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ContainsAny"></a>

## func [ContainsAny](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L82>)

```go
func ContainsAny(b []byte, chars string) bool
```

ContainsAny reports whether any of the UTF\-8\-encoded code points in chars are within b.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsAny([]byte("I like seafood."), "fÄo!"))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsAny([]byte("I like seafood."), "去是伟大的."))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsAny([]byte("I like seafood."), ""))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsAny([]byte(""), ""))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
true
false
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ContainsFunc"></a>

## func [ContainsFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L92>)

```go
func ContainsFunc(b []byte, f func(rune) bool) bool
```

ContainsFunc reports whether any of the UTF\-8\-encoded code points r within b satisfy f\(r\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	f := func(r rune) bool {
		return r >= 'a' && r <= 'z'
	}
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsFunc([]byte("HELLO"), f))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsFunc([]byte("World"), f))
}
```

#### Output

```go
false
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ContainsRune"></a>

## func [ContainsRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L87>)

```go
func ContainsRune(b []byte, r rune) bool
```

ContainsRune reports whether the rune is contained in the UTF\-8\-encoded byte slice b.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsRune([]byte("I like seafood."), 'f'))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsRune([]byte("I like seafood."), 'ö'))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsRune([]byte("去是伟大的!"), '大'))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsRune([]byte("去是伟大的!"), '!'))
	fmt.Println(bytes.ContainsRune([]byte(""), '@'))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
true
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Count"></a>

## func [Count](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L57>)

```go
func Count(s, sep []byte) int
```

Count counts the number of non\-overlapping instances of sep in s. If sep is an empty slice, Count returns 1 \+ the number of UTF\-8\-encoded code points in s.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.Count([]byte("cheese"), []byte("e")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.Count([]byte("five"), []byte(""))) // before & after each rune
}
```

#### Output

```go
3
5
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Cut"></a>

## func [Cut](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1374>)

```go
func Cut(s, sep []byte) (before, after []byte, found bool)
```

Cut slices s around the first instance of sep, returning the text before and after sep. The found result reports whether sep appears in s. If sep does not appear in s, cut returns s, nil, false.

Cut returns slices of the original slice s, not copies.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	show := func(s, sep string) {
		before, after, found := bytes.Cut([]byte(s), []byte(sep))
		fmt.Printf("Cut(%q, %q) = %q, %q, %v\n", s, sep, before, after, found)
	}
	show("Gopher", "Go")
	show("Gopher", "ph")
	show("Gopher", "er")
	show("Gopher", "Badger")
}
```

#### Output

```go
Cut("Gopher", "Go") = "", "pher", true
Cut("Gopher", "ph") = "Go", "er", true
Cut("Gopher", "er") = "Goph", "", true
Cut("Gopher", "Badger") = "Gopher", "", false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="CutPrefix"></a>

## func [CutPrefix](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1397>)

```go
func CutPrefix(s, prefix []byte) (after []byte, found bool)
```

CutPrefix returns s without the provided leading prefix byte slice and reports whether it found the prefix. If s doesn't start with prefix, CutPrefix returns s, false. If prefix is the empty byte slice, CutPrefix returns s, true.

CutPrefix returns slices of the original slice s, not copies.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	show := func(s, prefix string) {
		after, found := bytes.CutPrefix([]byte(s), []byte(prefix))
		fmt.Printf("CutPrefix(%q, %q) = %q, %v\n", s, prefix, after, found)
	}
	show("Gopher", "Go")
	show("Gopher", "ph")
}
```

#### Output

```go
CutPrefix("Gopher", "Go") = "pher", true
CutPrefix("Gopher", "ph") = "Gopher", false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="CutSuffix"></a>

## func [CutSuffix](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1410>)

```go
func CutSuffix(s, suffix []byte) (before []byte, found bool)
```

CutSuffix returns s without the provided ending suffix byte slice and reports whether it found the suffix. If s doesn't end with suffix, CutSuffix returns s, false. If suffix is the empty byte slice, CutSuffix returns s, true.

CutSuffix returns slices of the original slice s, not copies.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	show := func(s, suffix string) {
		before, found := bytes.CutSuffix([]byte(s), []byte(suffix))
		fmt.Printf("CutSuffix(%q, %q) = %q, %v\n", s, suffix, before, found)
	}
	show("Gopher", "Go")
	show("Gopher", "er")
}
```

#### Output

```go
CutSuffix("Gopher", "Go") = "Gopher", false
CutSuffix("Gopher", "er") = "Goph", true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Equal"></a>

## func [Equal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L20>)

```go
func Equal(a, b []byte) bool
```

Equal reports whether a and b are the same length and contain the same bytes. A nil argument is equivalent to an empty slice.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.Equal([]byte("Go"), []byte("Go")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.Equal([]byte("Go"), []byte("C++")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="EqualFold"></a>

## func [EqualFold](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1206>)

```go
func EqualFold(s, t []byte) bool
```

EqualFold reports whether s and t, interpreted as UTF\-8 strings, are equal under simple Unicode case\-folding, which is a more general form of case\-insensitivity.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.EqualFold([]byte("Go"), []byte("go")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Fields"></a>

## func [Fields](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L457>)

```go
func Fields(s []byte) [][]byte
```

Fields interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded code points. It splits the slice s around each instance of one or more consecutive white space characters, as defined by [unicode.IsSpace](<https://pkg.go.dev/unicode/#IsSpace>), returning a slice of subslices of s or an empty slice if s contains only white space. Every element of the returned slice is non\-empty. Unlike [Split](<#Split>), leading and trailing runs of white space characters are discarded.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("Fields are: %q", bytes.Fields([]byte("  foo bar  baz   ")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
Fields are: ["foo" "bar" "baz"]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FieldsFunc"></a>

## func [FieldsFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L516>)

```go
func FieldsFunc(s []byte, f func(rune) bool) [][]byte
```

FieldsFunc interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded code points. It splits the slice s at each run of code points c satisfying f\(c\) and returns a slice of subslices of s. If all code points in s satisfy f\(c\), or len\(s\) == 0, an empty slice is returned. Every element of the returned slice is non\-empty. Unlike [Split](<#Split>), leading and trailing runs of code points satisfying f\(c\) are discarded.

FieldsFunc makes no guarantees about the order in which it calls f\(c\) and assumes that f always returns the same value for a given c.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	f := func(c rune) bool {
		return !unicode.IsLetter(c) && !unicode.IsNumber(c)
	}
	fmt.Printf("Fields are: %q", bytes.FieldsFunc([]byte("  foo1;bar2,baz3..."), f))
}
```

#### Output

```go
Fields are: ["foo1" "bar2" "baz3"]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FieldsFuncSeq"></a>

## func [FieldsFuncSeq](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/iter.go#L116>)

```go
func FieldsFuncSeq(s []byte, f func(rune) bool) iter.Seq[[]byte]
```

FieldsFuncSeq returns an iterator over subslices of s split around runs of Unicode code points satisfying f\(c\). The iterator yields the same subslices that would be returned by [FieldsFunc](<#FieldsFunc>)\(s\), but without constructing a new slice containing the subslices.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	text := []byte("The quick brown fox")
	fmt.Println("Split on whitespace(similar to FieldsSeq):")
	for word := range bytes.FieldsFuncSeq(text, unicode.IsSpace) {
		fmt.Printf("%q\n", word)
	}

	mixedText := []byte("abc123def456ghi")
	fmt.Println("\nSplit on digits:")
	for word := range bytes.FieldsFuncSeq(mixedText, unicode.IsDigit) {
		fmt.Printf("%q\n", word)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
Split on whitespace(similar to FieldsSeq):
"The"
"quick"
"brown"
"fox"

Split on digits:
"abc"
"def"
"ghi"
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FieldsSeq"></a>

## func [FieldsSeq](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/iter.go#L83>)

```go
func FieldsSeq(s []byte) iter.Seq[[]byte]
```

FieldsSeq returns an iterator over subslices of s split around runs of whitespace characters, as defined by [unicode.IsSpace](<https://pkg.go.dev/unicode/#IsSpace>). The iterator yields the same subslices that would be returned by [Fields](<#Fields>)\(s\), but without constructing a new slice containing the subslices.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	text := []byte("The quick brown fox")
	fmt.Println("Split byte slice into fields:")
	for word := range bytes.FieldsSeq(text) {
		fmt.Printf("%q\n", word)
	}

	textWithSpaces := []byte("  lots   of   spaces  ")
	fmt.Println("\nSplit byte slice with multiple spaces:")
	for word := range bytes.FieldsSeq(textWithSpaces) {
		fmt.Printf("%q\n", word)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
Split byte slice into fields:
"The"
"quick"
"brown"
"fox"

Split byte slice with multiple spaces:
"lots"
"of"
"spaces"
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="HasPrefix"></a>

## func [HasPrefix](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L594>)

```go
func HasPrefix(s, prefix []byte) bool
```

HasPrefix reports whether the byte slice s begins with prefix.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.HasPrefix([]byte("Gopher"), []byte("Go")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.HasPrefix([]byte("Gopher"), []byte("C")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.HasPrefix([]byte("Gopher"), []byte("")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="HasSuffix"></a>

## func [HasSuffix](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L599>)

```go
func HasSuffix(s, suffix []byte) bool
```

HasSuffix reports whether the byte slice s ends with suffix.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.HasSuffix([]byte("Amigo"), []byte("go")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.HasSuffix([]byte("Amigo"), []byte("O")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.HasSuffix([]byte("Amigo"), []byte("Ami")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.HasSuffix([]byte("Amigo"), []byte("")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
false
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Index"></a>

## func [Index](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1281>)

```go
func Index(s, sep []byte) int
```

Index returns the index of the first instance of sep in s, or \-1 if sep is not present in s.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.Index([]byte("chicken"), []byte("ken")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.Index([]byte("chicken"), []byte("dmr")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
4
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="IndexAny"></a>

## func [IndexAny](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L221>)

```go
func IndexAny(s []byte, chars string) int
```

IndexAny interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded Unicode code points. It returns the byte index of the first occurrence in s of any of the Unicode code points in chars. It returns \-1 if chars is empty or if there is no code point in common.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexAny([]byte("chicken"), "aeiouy"))
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexAny([]byte("crwth"), "aeiouy"))
}
```

#### Output

```go
2
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="IndexByte"></a>

## func [IndexByte](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L97>)

```go
func IndexByte(b []byte, c byte) int
```

IndexByte returns the index of the first instance of c in b, or \-1 if c is not present in b.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexByte([]byte("chicken"), byte('k')))
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexByte([]byte("chicken"), byte('g')))
}
```

#### Output

```go
4
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="IndexFunc"></a>

## func [IndexFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L895>)

```go
func IndexFunc(s []byte, f func(r rune) bool) int
```

IndexFunc interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded code points. It returns the byte index in s of the first Unicode code point satisfying f\(c\), or \-1 if none do.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	f := func(c rune) bool {
		return unicode.Is(unicode.Han, c)
	}
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexFunc([]byte("Hello, 世界"), f))
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexFunc([]byte("Hello, world"), f))
}
```

#### Output

```go
7
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="IndexRune"></a>

## func [IndexRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L139>)

```go
func IndexRune(s []byte, r rune) int
```

IndexRune interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded code points. It returns the byte index of the first occurrence in s of the given rune. It returns \-1 if rune is not present in s. If r is \[utf8.RuneError\], it returns the first instance of any invalid UTF\-8 byte sequence.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexRune([]byte("chicken"), 'k'))
	fmt.Println(bytes.IndexRune([]byte("chicken"), 'd'))
}
```

#### Output

```go
4
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Join"></a>

## func [Join](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L561>)

```go
func Join(s [][]byte, sep []byte) []byte
```

Join concatenates the elements of s to create a new byte slice. The separator sep is placed between elements in the resulting slice.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	s := [][]byte{[]byte("foo"), []byte("bar"), []byte("baz")}
	fmt.Printf("%s", bytes.Join(s, []byte(", ")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
foo, bar, baz
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="LastIndex"></a>

## func [LastIndex](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L111>)

```go
func LastIndex(s, sep []byte) int
```

LastIndex returns the index of the last instance of sep in s, or \-1 if sep is not present in s.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.Index([]byte("go gopher"), []byte("go")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndex([]byte("go gopher"), []byte("go")))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndex([]byte("go gopher"), []byte("rodent")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
0
3
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="LastIndexAny"></a>

## func [LastIndexAny](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L299>)

```go
func LastIndexAny(s []byte, chars string) int
```

LastIndexAny interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded Unicode code points. It returns the byte index of the last occurrence in s of any of the Unicode code points in chars. It returns \-1 if chars is empty or if there is no code point in common.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexAny([]byte("go gopher"), "MüQp"))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexAny([]byte("go 地鼠"), "地大"))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexAny([]byte("go gopher"), "z,!."))
}
```

#### Output

```go
5
3
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="LastIndexByte"></a>

## func [LastIndexByte](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L130>)

```go
func LastIndexByte(s []byte, c byte) int
```

LastIndexByte returns the index of the last instance of c in s, or \-1 if c is not present in s.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexByte([]byte("go gopher"), byte('g')))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexByte([]byte("go gopher"), byte('r')))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexByte([]byte("go gopher"), byte('z')))
}
```

#### Output

```go
3
8
-1
```

</p>

<a name="LastIndexFunc"></a>

## func [LastIndexFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L902>)

```go
func LastIndexFunc(s []byte, f func(r rune) bool) int
```

LastIndexFunc interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded code points. It returns the byte index in s of the last Unicode code point satisfying f\(c\), or \-1 if none do.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexFunc([]byte("go gopher!"), unicode.IsLetter))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexFunc([]byte("go gopher!"), unicode.IsPunct))
	fmt.Println(bytes.LastIndexFunc([]byte("go gopher!"), unicode.IsNumber))
}
```

#### Output

```go
8
9
-1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Lines"></a>

## func [Lines](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/iter.go#L18>)

```go
func Lines(s []byte) iter.Seq[[]byte]
```

Lines returns an iterator over the newline\-terminated lines in the byte slice s. The lines yielded by the iterator include their terminating newlines. If s is empty, the iterator yields no lines at all. If s does not end in a newline, the final yielded line will not end in a newline. It returns a single\-use iterator.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	text := []byte("Hello\nWorld\nGo Programming\n")
	for line := range bytes.Lines(text) {
		fmt.Printf("%q\n", line)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
"Hello\n"
"World\n"
"Go Programming\n"
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Map"></a>

## func [Map](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L607>)

```go
func Map(mapping func(r rune) rune, s []byte) []byte
```

Map returns a copy of the byte slice s with all its characters modified according to the mapping function. If mapping returns a negative value, the character is dropped from the byte slice with no replacement. The characters in s and the output are interpreted as UTF\-8\-encoded code points.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	rot13 := func(r rune) rune {
		switch {
		case r >= 'A' && r <= 'Z':
			return 'A' + (r-'A'+13)%26
		case r >= 'a' && r <= 'z':
			return 'a' + (r-'a'+13)%26
		}
		return r
	}
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.Map(rot13, []byte("'Twas brillig and the slithy gopher...")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
'Gjnf oevyyvt naq gur fyvgul tbcure...
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Repeat"></a>

## func [Repeat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L639>)

```go
func Repeat(b []byte, count int) []byte
```

Repeat returns a new byte slice consisting of count copies of b.

It panics if count is negative or if the result of \(len\(b\) \* count\) overflows.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("ba%s", bytes.Repeat([]byte("na"), 2))
}
```

#### Output

```go
banana
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Replace"></a>

## func [Replace](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1155>)

```go
func Replace(s, old, new []byte, n int) []byte
```

Replace returns a copy of the slice s with the first n non\-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. If old is empty, it matches at the beginning of the slice and after each UTF\-8 sequence, yielding up to k\+1 replacements for a k\-rune slice. If n \< 0, there is no limit on the number of replacements.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.Replace([]byte("oink oink oink"), []byte("k"), []byte("ky"), 2))
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.Replace([]byte("oink oink oink"), []byte("oink"), []byte("moo"), -1))
}
```

#### Output

```go
oinky oinky oink
moo moo moo
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ReplaceAll"></a>

## func [ReplaceAll](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1199>)

```go
func ReplaceAll(s, old, new []byte) []byte
```

ReplaceAll returns a copy of the slice s with all non\-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. If old is empty, it matches at the beginning of the slice and after each UTF\-8 sequence, yielding up to k\+1 replacements for a k\-rune slice.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.ReplaceAll([]byte("oink oink oink"), []byte("oink"), []byte("moo")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
moo moo moo
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Runes"></a>

## func [Runes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1137>)

```go
func Runes(s []byte) []rune
```

Runes interprets s as a sequence of UTF\-8\-encoded code points. It returns a slice of runes \(Unicode code points\) equivalent to s.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	rs := bytes.Runes([]byte("go gopher"))
	for _, r := range rs {
		fmt.Printf("%#U\n", r)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```go
U+0067 'g'
U+006F 'o'
U+0020 ' '
U+0067 'g'
U+006F 'o'
U+0070 'p'
U+0068 'h'
U+0065 'e'
U+0072 'r'
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Split"></a>

## func [Split](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L439>)

```go
func Split(s, sep []byte) [][]byte
```

Split slices s into all subslices separated by sep and returns a slice of the subslices between those separators. If sep is empty, Split splits after each UTF\-8 sequence. It is equivalent to SplitN with a count of \-1.

To split around the first instance of a separator, see [Cut](<#Cut>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%q\n", bytes.Split([]byte("a,b,c"), []byte(",")))
	fmt.Printf("%q\n", bytes.Split([]byte("a man a plan a canal panama"), []byte("a ")))
	fmt.Printf("%q\n", bytes.Split([]byte(" xyz "), []byte("")))
	fmt.Printf("%q\n", bytes.Split([]byte(""), []byte("Bernardo O'Higgins")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
["a" "b" "c"]
["" "man " "plan " "canal panama"]
[" " "x" "y" "z" " "]
[""]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SplitAfter"></a>

## func [SplitAfter](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L445>)

```go
func SplitAfter(s, sep []byte) [][]byte
```

SplitAfter slices s into all subslices after each instance of sep and returns a slice of those subslices. If sep is empty, SplitAfter splits after each UTF\-8 sequence. It is equivalent to SplitAfterN with a count of \-1.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%q\n", bytes.SplitAfter([]byte("a,b,c"), []byte(",")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
["a," "b," "c"]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SplitAfterN"></a>

## func [SplitAfterN](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L429>)

```go
func SplitAfterN(s, sep []byte, n int) [][]byte
```

SplitAfterN slices s into subslices after each instance of sep and returns a slice of those subslices. If sep is empty, SplitAfterN splits after each UTF\-8 sequence. The count determines the number of subslices to return:

- n \> 0: at most n subslices; the last subslice will be the unsplit remainder;
- n == 0: the result is nil \(zero subslices\);
- n \< 0: all subslices.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%q\n", bytes.SplitAfterN([]byte("a,b,c"), []byte(","), 2))
}
```

#### Output

```go
["a," "b,c"]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SplitAfterSeq"></a>

## func [SplitAfterSeq](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/iter.go#L75>)

```go
func SplitAfterSeq(s, sep []byte) iter.Seq[[]byte]
```

SplitAfterSeq returns an iterator over subslices of s split after each instance of sep. The iterator yields the same subslices that would be returned by [SplitAfter](<#SplitAfter>)\(s, sep\), but without constructing a new slice containing the subslices. It returns a single\-use iterator.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	s := []byte("a,b,c,d")
	for part := range bytes.SplitAfterSeq(s, []byte(",")) {
		fmt.Printf("%q\n", part)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
"a,"
"b,"
"c,"
"d"
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SplitN"></a>

## func [SplitN](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L420>)

```go
func SplitN(s, sep []byte, n int) [][]byte
```

SplitN slices s into subslices separated by sep and returns a slice of the subslices between those separators. If sep is empty, SplitN splits after each UTF\-8 sequence. The count determines the number of subslices to return:

- n \> 0: at most n subslices; the last subslice will be the unsplit remainder;
- n == 0: the result is nil \(zero subslices\);
- n \< 0: all subslices.

To split around the first instance of a separator, see [Cut](<#Cut>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%q\n", bytes.SplitN([]byte("a,b,c"), []byte(","), 2))
	z := bytes.SplitN([]byte("a,b,c"), []byte(","), 0)
	fmt.Printf("%q (nil = %v)\n", z, z == nil)
}
```

#### Output

```go
["a" "b,c"]
[] (nil = true)
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SplitSeq"></a>

## func [SplitSeq](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/iter.go#L67>)

```go
func SplitSeq(s, sep []byte) iter.Seq[[]byte]
```

SplitSeq returns an iterator over all subslices of s separated by sep. The iterator yields the same subslices that would be returned by [Split](<#Split>)\(s, sep\), but without constructing a new slice containing the subslices. It returns a single\-use iterator.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	s := []byte("a,b,c,d")
	for part := range bytes.SplitSeq(s, []byte(",")) {
		fmt.Printf("%q\n", part)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
"a"
"b"
"c"
"d"
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Title"></a>

## func [Title](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L828>)

```go
func Title(s []byte) []byte
```

Title treats s as UTF\-8\-encoded bytes and returns a copy with all Unicode letters that begin words mapped to their title case.

Deprecated: The rule Title uses for word boundaries does not handle Unicode punctuation properly. Use golang.org/x/text/cases instead.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s", bytes.Title([]byte("her royal highness")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
Her Royal Highness
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ToLower"></a>

## func [ToLower](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L720>)

```go
func ToLower(s []byte) []byte
```

ToLower returns a copy of the byte slice s with all Unicode letters mapped to their lower case.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s", bytes.ToLower([]byte("Gopher")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
gopher
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ToLowerSpecial"></a>

## func [ToLowerSpecial](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L759>)

```go
func ToLowerSpecial(c unicode.SpecialCase, s []byte) []byte
```

ToLowerSpecial treats s as UTF\-8\-encoded bytes and returns a copy with all the Unicode letters mapped to their lower case, giving priority to the special casing rules.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	str := []byte("AHOJ VÝVOJÁRİ GOLANG")
	totitle := bytes.ToLowerSpecial(unicode.AzeriCase, str)
	fmt.Println("Original : " + string(str))
	fmt.Println("ToLower : " + string(totitle))
}
```

#### Output

```go
Original : AHOJ VÝVOJÁRİ GOLANG
ToLower : ahoj vývojári golang
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ToTitle"></a>

## func [ToTitle](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L749>)

```go
func ToTitle(s []byte) []byte
```

ToTitle treats s as UTF\-8\-encoded bytes and returns a copy with all the Unicode letters mapped to their title case.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.ToTitle([]byte("loud noises")))
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.ToTitle([]byte("брат")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
LOUD NOISES
БРАТ
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ToTitleSpecial"></a>

## func [ToTitleSpecial](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L765>)

```go
func ToTitleSpecial(c unicode.SpecialCase, s []byte) []byte
```

ToTitleSpecial treats s as UTF\-8\-encoded bytes and returns a copy with all the Unicode letters mapped to their title case, giving priority to the special casing rules.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	str := []byte("ahoj vývojári golang")
	totitle := bytes.ToTitleSpecial(unicode.AzeriCase, str)
	fmt.Println("Original : " + string(str))
	fmt.Println("ToTitle : " + string(totitle))
}
```

#### Output

```go
Original : ahoj vývojári golang
ToTitle : AHOJ VÝVOJÁRİ GOLANG
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ToUpper"></a>

## func [ToUpper](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L689>)

```go
func ToUpper(s []byte) []byte
```

ToUpper returns a copy of the byte slice s with all Unicode letters mapped to their upper case.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s", bytes.ToUpper([]byte("Gopher")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
GOPHER
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ToUpperSpecial"></a>

## func [ToUpperSpecial](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L753>)

```go
func ToUpperSpecial(c unicode.SpecialCase, s []byte) []byte
```

ToUpperSpecial treats s as UTF\-8\-encoded bytes and returns a copy with all the Unicode letters mapped to their upper case, giving priority to the special casing rules.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	str := []byte("ahoj vývojári golang")
	totitle := bytes.ToUpperSpecial(unicode.AzeriCase, str)
	fmt.Println("Original : " + string(str))
	fmt.Println("ToUpper : " + string(totitle))
}
```

```go
ToUpper : AHOJ VÝVOJÁRİ GOLANG
```

<a name="ToValidUTF8"></a>

## func [ToValidUTF8](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L771>)

```go
func ToValidUTF8(s, replacement []byte) []byte
```

ToValidUTF8 treats s as UTF\-8\-encoded bytes and returns a copy with each run of bytes representing invalid UTF\-8 replaced with the bytes in replacement, which may be empty.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.ToValidUTF8([]byte("abc"), []byte("\uFFFD")))
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.ToValidUTF8([]byte("a\xffb\xC0\xAFc\xff"), []byte("")))
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", bytes.ToValidUTF8([]byte("\xed\xa0\x80"), []byte("abc")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
abc
abc
abc
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Trim"></a>

## func [Trim](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L980>)

```go
func Trim(s []byte, cutset string) []byte
```

Trim returns a subslice of s by slicing off all leading and trailing UTF\-8\-encoded code points contained in cutset.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("[%q]", bytes.Trim([]byte(" !!! Achtung! Achtung! !!! "), "! "))
}
```

#### Output

```go
["Achtung! Achtung"]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TrimFunc"></a>

## func [TrimFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L870>)

```go
func TrimFunc(s []byte, f func(r rune) bool) []byte
```

TrimFunc returns a subslice of s by slicing off all leading and trailing UTF\-8\-encoded code points c that satisfy f\(c\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimFunc([]byte("go-gopher!"), unicode.IsLetter)))
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimFunc([]byte("\"go-gopher!\""), unicode.IsLetter)))
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimFunc([]byte("go-gopher!"), unicode.IsPunct)))
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimFunc([]byte("1234go-gopher!567"), unicode.IsNumber)))
}
```

#### Output

```go
-gopher!
"go-gopher!"
go-gopher
go-gopher!
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TrimLeft"></a>

## func [TrimLeft](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L999>)

```go
func TrimLeft(s []byte, cutset string) []byte
```

TrimLeft returns a subslice of s by slicing off all leading UTF\-8\-encoded code points contained in cutset.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Print(string(bytes.TrimLeft([]byte("453gopher8257"), "0123456789")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
gopher8257
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TrimLeftFunc"></a>

## func [TrimLeftFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L847>)

```go
func TrimLeftFunc(s []byte, f func(r rune) bool) []byte
```

TrimLeftFunc treats s as UTF\-8\-encoded bytes and returns a subslice of s by slicing off all leading UTF\-8\-encoded code points c that satisfy f\(c\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimLeftFunc([]byte("go-gopher"), unicode.IsLetter)))
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimLeftFunc([]byte("go-gopher!"), unicode.IsPunct)))
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimLeftFunc([]byte("1234go-gopher!567"), unicode.IsNumber)))
}
```


<a name="TrimPrefix"></a>

## func [TrimPrefix](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L876>)

```go
func TrimPrefix(s, prefix []byte) []byte
```

TrimPrefix returns s without the provided leading prefix string. If s doesn't start with prefix, s is returned unchanged.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var b = []byte("Goodbye,, world!")
	b = bytes.TrimPrefix(b, []byte("Goodbye,"))
	b = bytes.TrimPrefix(b, []byte("See ya,"))
	fmt.Printf("Hello%s", b)
}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello, world!
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TrimRight"></a>

## func [TrimRight](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1058>)

```go
func TrimRight(s []byte, cutset string) []byte
```

TrimRight returns a subslice of s by slicing off all trailing UTF\-8\-encoded code points that are contained in cutset.

<details><summary>Example</summary>

```go

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Print(string(bytes.TrimRight([]byte("453gopher8257"), "0123456789")))
}
```

#### Output

```go
453gopher
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TrimRightFunc"></a>

## func [TrimRightFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L857>)

```go
func TrimRightFunc(s []byte, f func(r rune) bool) []byte
```

TrimRightFunc returns a subslice of s by slicing off all trailing UTF\-8\-encoded code points c that satisfy f\(c\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"unicode"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimRightFunc([]byte("go-gopher"), unicode.IsLetter)))
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimRightFunc([]byte("go-gopher!"), unicode.IsPunct)))
	fmt.Println(string(bytes.TrimRightFunc([]byte("1234go-gopher!567"), unicode.IsNumber)))
}
```

```go
go-

1234go-gopher!
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TrimSpace"></a>

## func [TrimSpace](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L1104>)

```go
func TrimSpace(s []byte) []byte
```

TrimSpace returns a subslice of s by slicing off all leading and trailing white space, as defined by Unicode.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Printf("%s", bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(" \t\n a lone gopher \n\t\r\n")))
}
```

#### Output

```go

```

</details>

<a name="TrimSuffix"></a>

## func [TrimSuffix](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/bytes.go#L885>)

```go
func TrimSuffix(s, suffix []byte) []byte
```

TrimSuffix returns s without the provided trailing suffix string. If s doesn't end with suffix, s is returned unchanged.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	var b = []byte("Hello, goodbye, etc!")
	b = bytes.TrimSuffix(b, []byte("goodbye, etc!"))
	b = bytes.TrimSuffix(b, []byte("gopher"))
	b = append(b, bytes.TrimSuffix([]byte("world!"), []byte("x!"))...)
	os.Stdout.Write(b)
}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello, world!
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer"></a>

## type [Buffer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L20-L30>)

A Buffer is a variable\-sized buffer of bytes with [Buffer.Read](<#Buffer.Read>) and [Buffer.Write](<#Buffer.Write>) methods. The zero value for Buffer is an empty buffer ready to use.

```go
type Buffer struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	var b bytes.Buffer // A Buffer needs no initialization.
	b.Write([]byte("Hello "))
	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "world!")
	b.WriteTo(os.Stdout)
}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello world!
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Reader)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/base64"
	"io"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	// A Buffer can turn a string or a []byte into an io.Reader.
	buf := bytes.NewBufferString("R29waGVycyBydWxlIQ==")
	dec := base64.NewDecoder(base64.StdEncoding, buf)
	io.Copy(os.Stdout, dec)
}
```

#### Output

```go
Gophers rule!
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewBuffer"></a>

### func [NewBuffer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L490>)

```go
func NewBuffer(buf []byte) *Buffer
```

NewBuffer creates and initializes a new [Buffer](<#Buffer>) using buf as its initial contents. The new [Buffer](<#Buffer>) takes ownership of buf, and the caller should not use buf after this call. NewBuffer is intended to prepare a [Buffer](<#Buffer>) to read existing data. It can also be used to set the initial size of the internal buffer for writing. To do that, buf should have the desired capacity but a length of zero.

In most cases, new\([Buffer](<#Buffer>)\) \(or just declaring a [Buffer](<#Buffer>) variable\) is sufficient to initialize a [Buffer](<#Buffer>).

<a name="NewBufferString"></a>

### func [NewBufferString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L498>)

```go
func NewBufferString(s string) *Buffer
```

NewBufferString creates and initializes a new [Buffer](<#Buffer>) using string s as its initial contents. It is intended to prepare a buffer to read an existing string.

In most cases, new\([Buffer](<#Buffer>)\) \(or just declaring a [Buffer](<#Buffer>) variable\) is sufficient to initialize a [Buffer](<#Buffer>).

<a name="Buffer.Available"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Available](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L104>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Available() int
```

Available returns how many bytes are unused in the buffer.

<a name="Buffer.AvailableBuffer"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [AvailableBuffer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L66>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) AvailableBuffer() []byte
```

AvailableBuffer returns an empty buffer with b.Available\(\) capacity. This buffer is intended to be appended to and passed to an immediately succeeding [Buffer.Write](<#Buffer.Write>) call. The buffer is only valid until the next write operation on b.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
	"strconv"
)

func main() {
	var buf bytes.Buffer
	for i := 0; i < 4; i++ {
		b := buf.AvailableBuffer()
		b = strconv.AppendInt(b, int64(i), 10)
		b = append(b, ' ')
		buf.Write(b)
	}
	os.Stdout.Write(buf.Bytes())
}
```

#### Output

```go
0 1 2 3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.Bytes"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Bytes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L60>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Bytes() []byte
```

Bytes returns a slice of length b.Len\(\) holding the unread portion of the buffer. The slice is valid for use only until the next buffer modification \(that is, only until the next call to a method like [Buffer.Read](<#Buffer.Read>), [Buffer.Write](<#Buffer.Write>), [Buffer.Reset](<#Buffer.Reset>), or [Buffer.Truncate](<#Buffer.Truncate>)\). The slice aliases the buffer content at least until the next buffer modification, so immediate changes to the slice will affect the result of future reads.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	buf := bytes.Buffer{}
	buf.Write([]byte{'h', 'e', 'l', 'l', 'o', ' ', 'w', 'o', 'r', 'l', 'd'})
	os.Stdout.Write(buf.Bytes())
}
```

#### Output

```go
hello world
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.Cap"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Cap](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L101>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Cap() int
```

Cap returns the capacity of the buffer's underlying byte slice, that is, the total space allocated for the buffer's data.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	buf1 := bytes.NewBuffer(make([]byte, 10))
	buf2 := bytes.NewBuffer(make([]byte, 0, 10))
	fmt.Println(buf1.Cap())
	fmt.Println(buf2.Cap())
}
```

#### Output

```go
10
10
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.Grow"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Grow](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L182>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Grow(n int)
```

Grow grows the buffer's capacity, if necessary, to guarantee space for another n bytes. After Grow\(n\), at least n bytes can be written to the buffer without another allocation. If n is negative, Grow will panic. If the buffer can't grow it will panic with [ErrTooLarge](<#ErrTooLarge>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var b bytes.Buffer
	b.Grow(64)
	bb := b.Bytes()
	b.Write([]byte("64 bytes or fewer"))
	fmt.Printf("%q", bb[:b.Len()])
}
```

#### Output

```go
"64 bytes or fewer"
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.Len"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L97>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of bytes of the unread portion of the buffer; b.Len\(\) == len\(b.Bytes\(\)\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var b bytes.Buffer
	b.Grow(64)
	b.Write([]byte("abcde"))
	fmt.Printf("%d", b.Len())
}
```

#### Output

```go
5
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.Next"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Next](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L358>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Next(n int) []byte
```

Next returns a slice containing the next n bytes from the buffer, advancing the buffer as if the bytes had been returned by [Buffer.Read](<#Buffer.Read>). If there are fewer than n bytes in the buffer, Next returns the entire buffer. The slice is only valid until the next call to a read or write method.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var b bytes.Buffer
	b.Grow(64)
	b.Write([]byte("abcde"))
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", b.Next(2))
	fmt.Printf("%s\n", b.Next(2))
	fmt.Printf("%s", b.Next(2))
}
```

#### Output

```go
ab
cd
e
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.Peek"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Peek](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L85>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Peek(n int) ([]byte, error)
```

Peek returns the next n bytes without advancing the buffer. If Peek returns fewer than n bytes, it also returns [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>). The slice is only valid until the next call to a read or write method. The slice aliases the buffer content at least until the next buffer modification, so immediate changes to the slice will affect the result of future reads.

<a name="Buffer.Read"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Read](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L336>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read reads the next len\(p\) bytes from the buffer or until the buffer is drained. The return value n is the number of bytes read. If the buffer has no data to return, err is [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>) \(unless len\(p\) is zero\); otherwise it is nil.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var b bytes.Buffer
	b.Grow(64)
	b.Write([]byte("abcde"))
	rdbuf := make([]byte, 1)
	n, err := b.Read(rdbuf)
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
	fmt.Println(n)
	fmt.Println(b.String())
	fmt.Println(string(rdbuf))
}
```

#### Output

```go
1
bcde
a
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.ReadByte"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [ReadByte](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L374>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) ReadByte() (byte, error)
```

ReadByte reads and returns the next byte from the buffer. If no byte is available, it returns error [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	var b bytes.Buffer
	b.Grow(64)
	b.Write([]byte("abcde"))
	c, err := b.ReadByte()
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
	fmt.Println(c)
	fmt.Println(b.String())
}
```

#### Output

```go
97
bcde
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Buffer.ReadBytes"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [ReadBytes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L448>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) ReadBytes(delim byte) (line []byte, err error)
```

ReadBytes reads until the first occurrence of delim in the input, returning a slice containing the data up to and including the delimiter. If ReadBytes encounters an error before finding a delimiter, it returns the data read before the error and the error itself \(often [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>)\). ReadBytes returns err \!= nil if and only if the returned data does not end in delim.

<a name="Buffer.ReadFrom"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [ReadFrom](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L224>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) ReadFrom(r io.Reader) (n int64, err error)
```

ReadFrom reads data from r until EOF and appends it to the buffer, growing the buffer as needed. The return value n is the number of bytes read. Any error except io.EOF encountered during the read is also returned. If the buffer becomes too large, ReadFrom will panic with [ErrTooLarge](<#ErrTooLarge>).

<a name="Buffer.ReadRune"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [ReadRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L391>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) ReadRune() (r rune, size int, err error)
```

ReadRune reads and returns the next UTF\-8\-encoded Unicode code point from the buffer. If no bytes are available, the error returned is io.EOF. If the bytes are an erroneous UTF\-8 encoding, it consumes one byte and returns U\+FFFD, 1.

<a name="Buffer.ReadString"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [ReadString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L476>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) ReadString(delim byte) (line string, err error)
```

ReadString reads until the first occurrence of delim in the input, returning a string containing the data up to and including the delimiter. If ReadString encounters an error before finding a delimiter, it returns the data read before the error and the error itself \(often [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>)\). ReadString returns err \!= nil if and only if the returned data does not end in delim.

<a name="Buffer.Reset"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Reset](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L124>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Reset()
```

Reset resets the buffer to be empty, but it retains the underlying storage for use by future writes. Reset is the same as [Buffer.Truncate](<#Buffer.Truncate>)\(0\).

<a name="Buffer.String"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L72>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) String() string
```

String returns the contents of the unread portion of the buffer as a string. If the [Buffer](<#Buffer>) is a nil pointer, it returns "\<nil\>".

To build strings more efficiently, see the [strings.Builder](<https://pkg.go.dev/strings/#Builder>) type.

<a name="Buffer.Truncate"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Truncate](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L109>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Truncate(n int)
```

Truncate discards all but the first n unread bytes from the buffer but continues to use the same allocated storage. It panics if n is negative or greater than the length of the buffer.

<a name="Buffer.UnreadByte"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [UnreadByte](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L431>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) UnreadByte() error
```

UnreadByte unreads the last byte returned by the most recent successful read operation that read at least one byte. If a write has happened since the last read, if the last read returned an error, or if the read read zero bytes, UnreadByte returns an error.

<a name="Buffer.UnreadRune"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [UnreadRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L414>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) UnreadRune() error
```

UnreadRune unreads the last rune returned by [Buffer.ReadRune](<#Buffer.ReadRune>). If the most recent read or write operation on the buffer was not a successful [Buffer.ReadRune](<#Buffer.ReadRune>), UnreadRune returns an error. \(In this regard it is stricter than [Buffer.UnreadByte](<#Buffer.UnreadByte>), which will unread the last byte from any read operation.\)

<a name="Buffer.Write"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [Write](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L193>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Write appends the contents of p to the buffer, growing the buffer as needed. The return value n is the length of p; err is always nil. If the buffer becomes too large, Write will panic with [ErrTooLarge](<#ErrTooLarge>).

<a name="Buffer.WriteByte"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [WriteByte](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L303>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) WriteByte(c byte) error
```

WriteByte appends the byte c to the buffer, growing the buffer as needed. The returned error is always nil, but is included to match [bufio.Writer](<https://pkg.go.dev/bufio/#Writer>)'s WriteByte. If the buffer becomes too large, WriteByte will panic with [ErrTooLarge](<#ErrTooLarge>).

<a name="Buffer.WriteRune"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [WriteRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L317>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) WriteRune(r rune) (n int, err error)
```

WriteRune appends the UTF\-8 encoding of Unicode code point r to the buffer, returning its length and an error, which is always nil but is included to match [bufio.Writer](<https://pkg.go.dev/bufio/#Writer>)'s WriteRune. The buffer is grown as needed; if it becomes too large, WriteRune will panic with [ErrTooLarge](<#ErrTooLarge>).

<a name="Buffer.WriteString"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [WriteString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L205>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) WriteString(s string) (n int, err error)
```

WriteString appends the contents of s to the buffer, growing the buffer as needed. The return value n is the length of s; err is always nil. If the buffer becomes too large, WriteString will panic with [ErrTooLarge](<#ErrTooLarge>).

<a name="Buffer.WriteTo"></a>

### func \(\*Buffer\) [WriteTo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/buffer.go#L276>)

```go
func (b *Buffer) WriteTo(w io.Writer) (n int64, err error)
```

WriteTo writes data to w until the buffer is drained or an error occurs. The return value n is the number of bytes written; it always fits into an int, but it is int64 to match the [io.WriterTo](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#WriterTo>) interface. Any error encountered during the write is also returned.

<a name="Reader"></a>

## type [Reader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L18-L22>)

A Reader implements the [io.Reader](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#Reader>), [io.ReaderAt](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#ReaderAt>), [io.WriterTo](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#WriterTo>), [io.Seeker](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#Seeker>), [io.ByteScanner](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#ByteScanner>), and [io.RuneScanner](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#RuneScanner>) interfaces by reading from a byte slice. Unlike a [Buffer](<#Buffer>), a Reader is read\-only and supports seeking. The zero value for Reader operates like a Reader of an empty slice.

```go
type Reader struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewReader"></a>

### func [NewReader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L159>)

```go
func NewReader(b []byte) *Reader
```

NewReader returns a new [Reader](<#Reader>) reading from b.

<a name="Reader.Len"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L26>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of bytes of the unread portion of the slice.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(bytes.NewReader([]byte("Hi!")).Len())
	fmt.Println(bytes.NewReader([]byte("こんにちは!")).Len())
}
```

#### Output

```go
3
16
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Reader.Read"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Read](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L39>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Read(b []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read implements the [io.Reader](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#Reader>) interface.

<a name="Reader.ReadAt"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [ReadAt](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L50>)

```go
func (r *Reader) ReadAt(b []byte, off int64) (n int, err error)
```

ReadAt implements the [io.ReaderAt](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#ReaderAt>) interface.

<a name="Reader.ReadByte"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [ReadByte](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L66>)

```go
func (r *Reader) ReadByte() (byte, error)
```

ReadByte implements the [io.ByteReader](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#ByteReader>) interface.

<a name="Reader.ReadRune"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [ReadRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L87>)

```go
func (r *Reader) ReadRune() (ch rune, size int, err error)
```

ReadRune implements the [io.RuneReader](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#RuneReader>) interface.

<a name="Reader.Reset"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Reset](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L156>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Reset(b []byte)
```

Reset resets the [Reader](<#Reader>) to be reading from b.

<a name="Reader.Seek"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Seek](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L116>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Seek(offset int64, whence int) (int64, error)
```

Seek implements the [io.Seeker](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#Seeker>) interface.

<a name="Reader.Size"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Size](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L36>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Size() int64
```

Size returns the original length of the underlying byte slice. Size is the number of bytes available for reading via [Reader.ReadAt](<#Reader.ReadAt>). The result is unaffected by any method calls except [Reader.Reset](<#Reader.Reset>).

<a name="Reader.UnreadByte"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [UnreadByte](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/bytes/reader.go#L77>)

```go
func (r *Reader) UnreadByte() error
```

UnreadByte complements [Reader.ReadByte](<#Reader.ReadByte>) in implementing the [io.ByteScanner](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#ByteScanner>) interface.

<a name="Reader.UnreadRune"></a>

```go
func (r *Reader) UnreadRune() error
```

UnreadRune complements [Reader.ReadRune](<#Reader.ReadRune>) in implementing the [io.RuneScanner](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#RuneScanner>) interface.
